Running Ruby Meetups

A practical guide for starting and running local Ruby meetups, from the first awkward event to a sustainable community.

Why Bother

The case for local Ruby communities and what a healthy meetup ecosystem looks like.

Starting From Zero

Finding your first venue, first attendees, and first talk in the awkward early phase.

Finding Your Format

Talk-based, workshop, hack night, hybrid, and what works for each community size.

Getting People in the Room

Promotion, consistency, word of mouth, and what actually drives attendance.

Speakers & Talks

Sourcing talks, supporting first-time speakers, and keeping content fresh.

Money & Sponsors

Whether you need money, finding sponsors, keeping it simple, and backup plans.

The Logistics Nobody Talks About

Food, AV, accessibility, and the details that quietly make or break a night.

Keeping It Going

Burnout, co-organizers, handover, and long-term definitions of success.

Building Culture

Code of conduct, tone-setting, and making the meetup welcoming in practice.

Growing & Evolving

When to grow, when not to, spin-offs, and connecting to the wider Ruby world.

© 2026 Ruby Community Guides Part of RubyEvents
